title: Media Types, Content Codings, Character Encodings, and Source Identity · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs media types, content codings, character encodings, and source identity from revision, authorization, seed, URL, policy, response bytes, parsed identity, record provenance, frontier transition, Promise settlement, browser state, report row, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for byte-to-Unicode admission ledger after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Validate declared content codings and distinguish unsupported, corrupt, truncated, and over-budget representations.
Use a documented media-type and conservative sniff policy while rejecting binary, multipart, and ambiguous content.
Reconcile transport, BOM, HTML declarations, decoder support, replacement characters, and confidence with source offsets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript team handles case 7774 during an authorized municipal service audit. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
